Ristorante Del Pescatore — Zues, Albania

Our Story

A Fisherman's Legacy on the Buna River

Perched at the confluence of the Buna and Drin rivers, Ristorante Del Pescatore is more than a restaurant — it is a living expression of Albania's coastal soul. Every fish on your plate was swimming in local waters that morning. Every dish carries the memory of Italian technique and Albanian generosity.

🐟

Wild-Caught Daily

River carp, sea bass, eel — sourced from the Buna River and Adriatic, never frozen.

🍋

Complimentary Welcome & Farewell

Every guest receives warm homemade bread with 3 house dips, and a lemon sorbet to close the meal.

🌊

Unforgettable Views

Panoramic views of the river, Rozafa Castle, and the Albanian mountains from every window.


Signature Dishes

Crafted from Sea & River

Our kitchen honours the catch of the day with Italian precision and Albanian heart. Below are just a handful of the dishes our guests return for again and again.

Seafood · Signature
Levrek Zgare

Grilled sea bass, wild-caught from the Adriatic — served whole with lemon and olive oil. 450–500g of pure freshness, every time.

Pasta · Fan Favourite
Spageti me Karkaleca

Spaghetti with wild Adriatic prawns in a rich tomato-bisque sauce. One of our most-ordered dishes and a true showcase of the sea.

Seafood · Chef's Choice
Miks Fruta Deti të Skuqura

Our famous fried seafood platter — crispy calamari and golden shrimps served with house dipping sauces. Generosity on a plate.

Pasta · Classic Italian
Spageti me Karkaleca

Al dente spaghetti with whole Adriatic prawns in a golden bisque with cherry tomatoes and fresh basil. Pure Italian soul.

Fish · River Classic
Krap Zgare

Grilled Buna River carp — our most beloved local fish, golden and crisp outside, tender within. A taste of Shkodër on your plate.

Meat · For Meat Lovers
Fileto Viçi me Tartuf

Grilled veal fillet with black truffle sauce, served with roasted potatoes and cherry tomatoes. Elegant, rich, unforgettable.

The Experience

More Than a Meal

🍞
Free Welcome Antipasto

Warm homemade bread arrives with three house-made dipping sauces — beetroot, cream, and pumpkin. Our gift to you before you've even ordered.

🍋
Complimentary Lemon Sorbet

Every meal ends with a refreshing house lemon sorbet — a signature farewell that guests from 15+ countries have written home about.

🌊
River & Castle Views

Every table has a view of the Buna River. On clear days, Rozafa Castle and the Albanian Alps frame your dining experience like a painting.

🅿️
Free Parking & Accessibility

Ample free parking, valet available, wheelchair accessible entrance and seating, free Wi-Fi, and halal options. We thought of everything.

What Guests Say

Loved Across 15 Countries

4.8 stars from 261 Google reviews and counting. Our guests come from across Europe and the world — and many come back.

"Outstanding seafood — easily the best crudo in Shkodër. Every time I am in town, this is always one of my must-visit places. The crudo is exceptional, incredibly fresh."

★★★★★

"Absolutely amazing food and friendly staff. Brilliant money for value. I've been in Zues two days and already eaten there twice! Planning to go again tomorrow."

A Feast for the Eyes

From the river view to the plate — every detail of Del Pescatore is crafted with care.

All photos courtesy of Ristorante Del Pescatore and our guests. More on Google Maps & TripAdvisor.

Our Story

Born of the River

Where the Buna meets the Drin, and the kitchen meets the water.

Ristorante Del Pescatore — "The Fisherman's Restaurant" — stands at one of Albania's most quietly spectacular locations: the point where the Buna and Drin rivers converge on the edge of Zues, just outside Shkodër. The name is not metaphor. It is a promise.

The restaurant was built on a simple principle: cook what the river and sea give you, and do it with Italian precision and Albanian soul. Over the years it has grown from a local secret into an international destination, with guests arriving from across Europe, the Middle East, and beyond — many discovering it by chance on the road between Tirana and Montenegro.

What makes Del Pescatore different is not just the fish — though our sea bass, carp tav, and seafood risotto have earned the kind of loyalty that brings guests back on consecutive nights. It is the ritual of the meal: the arrival of warm homemade bread with three house-made dips before you've even ordered, and the complimentary lemon sorbet that arrives quietly at the end, as if to say the meal is not over until we say goodbye properly.

Our kitchen blends the traditions of two cuisines: Italian technique — risotto, linguine, the patient slow-cook — and Albanian directness: fresh, honest, never overcomplicated. The fish was swimming hours ago. The vegetables come from local farms. The wine list has been chosen to match the food, not impress a sommelier.

With a 4.8 rating from over 261 guests and reviews written in more than 15 languages, Del Pescatore has become one of the most recommended restaurants in northern Albania. We are proud of that — and determined to keep earning it.

What We Stand For

Three Commitments

Freshness Without Compromise

Every fish we serve was caught that day. We do not use frozen seafood. We buy from local fishermen on the Buna and source from the Adriatic daily. If we cannot serve it fresh, we do not serve it at all.

Hospitality as Heritage

Albanian hospitality — besa — is the belief that a guest is sacred. We take that seriously. Every guest receives our complimentary welcome antipasto and farewell sorbet. It is not a gimmick. It is who we are.

Value Without Pretension

At ALL 1,500–2,000 per person, we offer what many fine-dining restaurants charge three times more to deliver. We believe exceptional food should be accessible. We will never compromise quality to cut cost.

Location

Find Us on the Buna River

We are on the main road between Shkodër and Montenegro, right on the riverbank. Free parking for all guests. Open Mon & Wed–Sun, 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM. Closed Tuesdays.
